output.title: Neonatal/young-infant pathway required
output.detail: The built-in reference intervals begin at 6 months. Hemoglobin and MCV change rapidly in early infancy and depend on gestational/birth history.
actions: Use local age-in-days/weeks and gestational-age reference intervals.
Apply a neonatal/young-infant anemia pathway and involve neonatology/hematology as clinically indicated.

output.title: Outside built-in pediatric age range
output.detail: The built-in CBC intervals stop before 18 years. Supply local laboratory reference intervals and use an age-appropriate adolescent/adult pathway.
actions: Enter local hemoglobin, MCV, and RDW limits before interpreting the result.

output.detail: Anemia and/or morphology cannot be classified until hemoglobin and MCV limits are available.
actions: Enter the reporting laboratory’s lower hemoglobin and MCV lower/upper limits.

output.title: Potentially unstable symptomatic anemia
output.detail: Respiratory, neurologic, cardiovascular, syncopal, or hemodynamic red flags were selected.
actions: Use immediate bedside assessment and local emergency/escalation protocols.
Do not use this calculator to delay stabilization or specialist consultation.

output.title: Active major bleeding
output.detail: Active major bleeding was selected and requires real-time clinical management rather than calculator-based sequencing.
actions: Activate the local acute bleeding/resuscitation pathway and identify the source.

changeRationale: EP-4 governance backfill (wave0-safety-foundation, scripts/evidence/backfill-rule-governance.mjs): mechanically populates required rule-governance metadata across all 91 rules in one deterministic, re-runnable codemod. output.title: Hemoglobin is below 7 g/dL
output.detail: The AAP classifies hemoglobin <7 g/dL as severe iron deficiency anemia when IDA is the diagnosis. This is not a universal transfusion threshold and does not establish etiology.
actions: Perform urgent clinical assessment for symptoms, instability, bleeding, hemolysis, and alternative etiologies.
Use local hematology/transfusion protocols and clinician judgment.

changeRationale: EP-4 governance backfill (wave0-safety-foundation, scripts/evidence/backfill-rule-governance.mjs): mechanically populates required rule-governance metadata across all 91 rules in one deterministic, re-runnable codemod. output.title: Anemia with another cytopenia
output.detail: Leukopenia, neutropenia, or thrombocytopenia was flagged with anemia; this broadens concern beyond isolated nutritional anemia.
actions: Obtain prompt expert CBC differential and smear review.
Consider urgent pediatric hematology/oncology evaluation based on severity and clinical findings.

output.title: Blasts reported on peripheral smear
output.detail: Blasts require urgent expert confirmation and evaluation for marrow infiltration or hematologic malignancy.
actions: Contact pediatric hematology/oncology urgently and follow local critical-result procedures.

output.detail: Schistocytes plus thrombocytopenia, renal, or neurologic findings were selected.
actions: Use immediate local emergency/hematology/nephrology evaluation; confirm smear and obtain organ-injury/coagulation studies.

output.title: Blood lead level ≥45 µg/dL
output.detail: CDC guidance places this level in the highest action tier, with urgent confirmatory and toxicology/clinical management requirements.
actions: Contact a Pediatric Environmental Health Specialty Unit or Poison Control and use the CDC/local high-level lead pathway.
If the child is symptomatic or exposure is ongoing, evaluate urgently in an appropriate facility.

output.title: Blood lead level 20–44 µg/dL
output.detail: This level requires prompt venous confirmation if needed, environmental investigation, clinical evaluation, and scheduled follow-up under CDC/local guidance.
actions: Use the current CDC/local lead pathway and arrange environmental/public-health follow-up.

output.title: Ferritin is inflammation-confounded
output.detail: Ferritin is not low and CRP is elevated. A normal or high ferritin cannot reliably exclude iron deficiency in this context; use the full iron/inflammation pattern and, when available, the sTfR/log10(ferritin) index.

output.title: Low ferritin can complicate beta-thalassemia interpretation
output.detail: Iron deficiency can lower HbA2. A non-elevated HbA2 during iron deficiency should not be treated as definitive exclusion of beta-thalassemia trait.

output.title: Normal G6PD result may be falsely reassuring
output.detail: A normal assay during acute reticulocytosis/hemolysis or soon after transfusion may not exclude G6PD deficiency. Repeat quantitative testing at baseline if suspicion remains.

output.title: Elevated capillary lead screen requires venous confirmation
output.detail: The capillary blood lead level is at or above 3.5 µg/dL. CDC guidance calls for confirmatory venous testing on a level-dependent timeline.
actions: Arrange confirmatory venous testing using the current CDC/local timeline.
Take an environmental exposure history and assess for coexisting iron deficiency.
